name: check-uncommitted-diff-before-shipping-shared-worktree
tags: git status uncommitted shared worktree dev deploy unrelated changes investigate before shipping task:15ee2da3
body:
Before running a deploy/merge pipeline from a shared dev worktree, run `git status --short` and inspect any uncommitted diff that isn't part of the current task -- don't assume it's broken or yours to discard, and don't ship it blindly either. In this case an unrelated uncommitted change (removing a dead function in hooks/server.py) turned out to be the tail end of an already-completed, already-committed task (its caller had already moved to dispatcher.py) -- verified via `git log --grep=<referenced task/bug id>` before committing it alongside the new feature.

Why: shared dev worktrees accumulate loose ends across sessions; a stale uncommitted diff can be either a forgotten completion of finished work (safe to include) or a broken half-edit (unsafe to ship) -- they look identical from `git status` alone.

How to apply: when `git status`/`git diff` in a shared worktree shows changes you didn't just make, grep the referenced task/bug id (in comments/docstrings) against git log and the task tracker before deciding whether to commit it, revert it, or leave it -- never silently include or silently discard.
